mnemonics: Calibrate Your IT Engine (CYITE) for performance
Capacity Planning is a Cycle, Not a Checkbox (CPCNC)
keyPoints: Iteration involves repeatedly refining capacity models and forecasts based on new data and feedback.
Calibration is the process of adjusting the model to align its predictions with actual, observed performance metrics.
The process is cyclical, requiring continuous evaluation to account for changing business needs and technological advancements.
commonMistakes: Treating the initial capacity plan as a one-time, static project rather than an ongoing process.
Failing to recalibrate models after deployment, leading to inaccurate forecasts as the IT environment evolves.
Ignoring feedback loops from operational data and user experience, resulting in plans that quickly become obsolete.
